---
title: "Unit 1 Day 1"
format: html
---

This is a test!

# Header

## sub-header 

#### header level4

__Bolded text__

*italosized text*

What about this stuff?

```{python}
print("Hello world!")
print("Hello too!")
"Last word"

x = 4
x += 2
x
```

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *
LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

# what is a dataframe
```{python}
df = pl.DataFrame(
    np.array([
    [4, 5, 6],
    [7, 8, 9],
    [10, 11, 12]
    ]),
    schema=['a', 'b', 'c']
)
df

```

```{python}
import polars as pl

url = "https://byui-cse.github.io/cse110-course/lesson11/life-expectancy.csv"
df = pl.read_csv(url)
```

```{python}
#display to make it show both for run cell
display(df.columns)
df.shape
```


```{python}
with pl.Config(tbl_rows=30, tbl_cols=20):
    display(df.head(18))
```


```{python}
df.describe()
```

 # Subset parts of data


 ```{python}
 df['Entity']
 df[['Entity', 'Code', 'Year']]
 df[3:5]
 df.head()
 #chaining things together
 df[['Entity', 'Code', 'Year']][3:5]['Code']
 df
 ```

 ## rename a column 


 ```{python}
 df_clean = df.rename({'Life expectncy (years)'
 ':''life_yrs'})
 df_clean
 ```


 ```{python}
 df_clean2 = df
 df_clean2columns = ['Entity', 'Code', 'Year', 'Life']
 ```


 ```{python}
 df_clean2.head()
 ```


 ```{python}
 df_clean.select(['Entity', 'Code'])
 df_clean.select(['Entity', 'Code'])
 df_clean.select(pl.col('life_yrs') * 12)
 ```

# select a subset of rows

.select is for columns 
.filter is for rows
 ```{python}
 df_clean.filter(pl.col('Year') > 1999)
 df_clean.filter(pl.col('Year') <= 2000, pl.col('Year')>= 1980)
 ```


 ```{python}
 #drop function
 df_clean.drop(pl.col('Code'))
 ```
